summary: A retweet of the same 'One American Heritage' Department of Labor video: a cinematic, stylized montage of desaturated sepia/B&W historical paintings (Founding Fathers, colonial soldiers, gilded Washington equestrian statue, sailing ships, a young serviceman) with word-by-word animated captions and scored narration glorifying American ancestors. The mythologizing heritage-pride tone and slick motion graphics make it a produced patriotic spectacle, in scope for the youth-appeal genre-experiment category.

script.scene: Sepia/B&W painting of Founding-era figures; animated caption 'OUR ANCESTORS CLIMBED'; reverent scored narration begins.
Desaturated layered montage of colonial figures and a gilded statue; caption 'CROSSED DESERTS,' narration: 'crossed deserts...'
Ranks of colonial soldiers with Revolutionary flag and gold Washington equestrian statue; caption 'WIDE OPEN PLAINS.'
Close-up of a young serviceman gazing upward over a smoky battlefield; caption 'WHO NEVER GAVE UP.'
Gold Washington-on-horseback statue against a misty harbor with tall ship; caption 'EVER GAVE IN,' narration: 'who never ever gave in.'

notable_text: OUR ANCESTORS CLIMBED; CROSSED DESERTS; WIDE OPEN PLAINS; WHO NEVER GAVE UP; EVER GAVE IN; (tweet) One American Heritage.
